skill.name: pdf-processing
skill.description: Extract PDF text and tables, fill PDF forms, and merge or split documents using Python PDF libraries.
skill.summary: Process PDFs with extraction, form filling, merging, splitting, and basic validation workflows.
skill.author: Joseph OBrien

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many command execution patterns, but review found they are Markdown code fences around Python examples, not executable skill code. The weak cryptography alert at SKILL.md line 3 has no supporting evidence. The skill includes local file write examples for expected PDF outputs, so publication is acceptable with normal file overwrite caution.

low_findings.title: External Command Detections Are Markdown Fence False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported Ruby or shell backtick execution findings correspond to triple-backtick Markdown code fences around Python snippets. No shell commands, subprocess calls, or dynamic command construction were found in the cited content.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited locations are Markdown fenced Python examples, not runnable skill hooks. Review found no external command invocation or user-controlled shell execution in the files.

low_findings.title: Local File Writes in PDF Examples
low_findings.description: The skill shows Python examples that write filled PDFs, flattened PDFs, extracted text, and CSV tables to local output files. This is expected for PDF processing, but users should choose output paths carefully to avoid overwriting files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file write calls are explicit and easy to verify. They are legitimate examples for producing PDF and extraction outputs, with no evidence of hidden writes or data exfiltration.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Detection Has No Evidence
low_findings.description: The static weak cryptography alert points to the skill description line. That line describes PDF extraction and form filling, and no cryptographic algorithm usage is present there.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited line contains plain metadata text, not cryptographic code or algorithm names. No evidence found for weak cryptography in the reviewed files.

content.value_statement: PDF work often requires repeatable extraction, form filling, and document assembly. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code practical Python patterns for common PDF tasks.
actual_capabilities: Extract text from one page or every page of a PDF with pdfplumber.
Detect and extract tables from PDF pages for review or export.
Fill PDF form fields and flatten completed forms with pypdf patterns.
Merge several PDF files into one combined document.
Split a PDF by selecting page ranges into a new output file.
Handle empty pages and scanned PDFs with basic error checks.
limitations: OCR examples require external Tesseract and Poppler dependencies when used for scanned PDFs.
Table extraction accuracy depends on the PDF layout and source quality.
Form field names must match the PDF exactly before filling works reliably.
The skill provides code patterns, not a hosted PDF processing service.

use_cases.title: Extract report data
use_cases.description: Pull text and tables from PDF reports for analysis, summaries, or spreadsheet preparation.

use_cases.title: Prepare completed forms
use_cases.description: Inspect PDF form fields, fill required values, validate inputs, and flatten final documents.

use_cases.title: Assemble document packets
use_cases.description: Merge related PDF files or split selected page ranges for client-ready document sets.

prompt_templates.title: Extract basic text
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the pdf-processing skill to extract text from my PDF and summarize any pages that have no readable text.

prompt_templates.title: Convert tables for review
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the pdf-processing skill to find tables in this PDF and describe a clean CSV export plan.

prompt_templates.title: Fill a PDF form
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the pdf-processing skill to inspect this PDF form, list field names, validate my data, and create a filled copy.

prompt_templates.title: Build a document pipeline
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the pdf-processing skill to design a workflow that extracts key pages, merges attachments, and saves named output files.

output_examples.input: A monthly PDF report with narrative pages and several embedded tables.
output_examples.output: A page-by-page extraction summary, table locations, and a recommended CSV export for each detected table.

output_examples.input: A fillable PDF intake form and a list of client values.
output_examples.output: A field mapping checklist, validation notes for missing values, and a completed form output plan.

output_examples.input: Three PDFs that need to become one final packet.
output_examples.output: A merge order, output file name, and checks to confirm page count and document order.

best_practices: Work on copies of original PDFs before writing output files.
Inspect form field names before filling values automatically.
Test extraction on a few pages before processing large documents.
anti_patterns: Assuming scanned PDFs contain extractable text without OCR.
Overwriting original PDFs with generated output files.
Treating automatically extracted tables as final without reviewing accuracy.

faq.question: Can this skill read text from scanned PDFs?
faq.answer: It can guide OCR workflows, but scanned PDFs need OCR tools such as Tesseract and Poppler.

faq.question: Can it fill PDF forms?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes patterns to inspect fields, fill values, validate inputs, and flatten completed forms.

faq.question: Does it merge and split PDFs?
faq.answer: Yes. It shows pypdf workflows for combining documents and extracting selected page ranges.

faq.question: Will table extraction always be accurate?
faq.answer: No. Accuracy depends on the PDF layout, borders, spacing, and source quality.

faq.question: Does this skill send files to a server?
faq.answer: No evidence found. The reviewed content only shows local Python processing examples.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
